execution_summary: Built a Python 3.10-slim Docker image with numpy, pandas, scipy, and openpyxl. Started the container and verified the plan to run replication_data/analysis_Petersen2017_v7__py.py. Updated the Python analysis to search for Excel logs in /app/data and also /workspace/replication_data to match the mounted study structure. On first execution, a regex in filename matching failed to identify subjects, causing an empty DataFrame and a KeyError. I fixed the regex for .xlsx detection and re-executed. The script completed, estimating TVA parameters per subject and computing paired t-tests (NoCue vs Cue) for v and t0, saving outputs to /app/data. The results did not replicate the original direction or significance; both tests were non-significant with directions opposite to preregistration expectations.

code_executed.logs: n_subjects_total=48; n_subjects_used_v=48; n_subjects_used_t0=48; paired_t_v_NoCue_vs_Cue: t=-1.3326, p=0.1891; paired_t_t0_NoCue_vs_Cue: t=1.2330, p=0.2237. Outputs saved to /app/data/replication_tva_params.csv and /app/data/replication_ttests.json.
code_executed.environment: Python 3.10 (python:3.10-slim) with numpy>=1.24, pandas>=1.5, scipy>=1.10, openpyxl>=3.1

results.hypothesis_tested: In a single-letter recognition task with backward masking, trials preceded by an auditory alerting cue will show higher TVA processing speed v and a lower perceptual threshold t0 compared to trials without a cue (within-subject comparison).

findings_summary.outcome_name: Paired t-statistic for processing speed v (NoCue vs Cue)
Paired t-statistic for perceptual threshold t0 (NoCue vs Cue)

tables.table_description: Per-subject estimates of v, t0 (ms), and pg for Cue and NoCue conditions used in the paired tests.
JSON summary containing counts and paired t-test results for v and t0.
